reasons: The Ragdoll cat appears fully grown with a well-developed, thick, and fluffy coat, typical of a mature adult. Its body is sturdy and muscular, and its eyes are clear and bright without any signs of cloudiness associated with senior cats. There is no visible greying around the muzzle, and its posture is strong and alert. These features collectively suggest an adult cat in its prime, likely around 3 years (36 months) old.
